body:
status=open
reason=retryable - host /tmp is completely full (ENOSPC), so the Bash tool cannot even create its own sandbox working directory. Every Bash invocation in this session failed with: `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-c054124f-20260708T152700-e06bca00'`. This is exactly the failure mode the bead describes (claim-liveness heartbeat writes under /tmp/ddx-claim-heartbeats failing with the same ENOSPC), and it also blocks running `go test`, `go build`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, and `git commit` for this bead itself. A subagent spawned to double-check disk state hit the identical error, confirming this is host-level /tmp exhaustion, not a per-command fluke. No code changes were made because the fix (adding the claim-liveness heartbeat root to execution resource preflight in cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go, using a new exported helper in cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go) cannot be verified without a working Bash/go toolchain. Writing unverified Go changes without running `go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` would violate the "do not commit red code" requirement and the AC's explicit test-run requirements. Planned fix (for the retry attempt once /tmp has free space): 1. In cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go, add an exported function, e.g. `ClaimLivenessRoot(ddxDir string) string`, that returns `filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), claimLivenessNamespace, hex.EncodeToString(sha1sum(canonicalClaimRoot(ddxDir))))` — the directory portion of claimLivenessPath, without the per-bead-id filename. Refactor claimLivenessPath to call this helper to avoid duplicating the hash logic. 2. In cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go, add a `ClaimLivenessRoot` (or similar) field to ExecutionResourcePreflight, populate it in NewExecutionResourceChecker by calling bead.ClaimLivenessRoot(projectRoot's .ddx dir), and include it in allRoots() alongside tempRoot/evidenceRoots so it is subject to the same hard/soft inode and byte checks. 3. Add TestResourcePreflightIncludesClaimLivenessRoot (proves the root list from Check()/RootChecks includes the claim-liveness root) and TestResourcePreflightFailsWhenClaimLivenessRootBelowHardInodeMinimum (proves Check() returns a ResourceExhaustedError when that root's free inodes are below the hard minimum, using the existing RootProbe override seam) in cli/internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go. 4. Run `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestResourcePreflightIncludesClaimLivenessRoot|TestResourcePreflightFailsWhenClaimLivenessRootBelowHardInodeMinimum"`, then the full `./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...` suite, then `lefthook run pre-commit`, then commit. </content>
suggested_action=retry with a smart agent

no_changes
rationale: status: open
reason: retryable - host /tmp is completely full (ENOSPC), so the Bash tool cannot even create its own sandbox working directory. Every Bash invocation in this session failed with: `ENOSPC: no space left on device, mkdir '/tmp/claude-501/-Users-erik-Projects--ddx-exec-wt--execute-bead-wt-ddx-c054124f-20260708T152700-e06bca00'`. This is exactly the failure mode the bead describes (claim-liveness heartbeat writes under /tmp/ddx-claim-heartbeats failing with the same ENOSPC), and it also blocks running `go test`, `go build`, `lefthook run pre-commit`, and `git commit` for this bead itself. A subagent spawned to double-check disk state hit the identical error, confirming this is host-level /tmp exhaustion, not a per-command fluke.

No code changes were made because the fix (adding the claim-liveness heartbeat root to execution resource preflight in cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go, using a new exported helper in cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go) cannot be verified without a working Bash/go toolchain. Writing unverified Go changes without running `go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` would violate the "do not commit red code" requirement and the AC's explicit test-run requirements.

Planned fix (for the retry attempt once /tmp has free space):
1. In cli/internal/bead/claim_liveness.go, add an exported function, e.g. `ClaimLivenessRoot(ddxDir string) string`, that returns `filepath.Join(os.TempDir(), claimLivenessNamespace, hex.EncodeToString(sha1sum(canonicalClaimRoot(ddxDir))))` — the directory portion of claimLivenessPath, without the per-bead-id filename. Refactor claimLivenessPath to call this helper to avoid duplicating the hash logic.
2. In cli/internal/agent/execution_resources.go, add a `ClaimLivenessRoot` (or similar) field to ExecutionResourcePreflight, populate it in NewExecutionResourceChecker by calling bead.ClaimLivenessRoot(projectRoot's .ddx dir), and include it in allRoots() alongside tempRoot/evidenceRoots so it is subject to the same hard/soft inode and byte checks.
3. Add TestResourcePreflightIncludesClaimLivenessRoot (proves the root list from Check()/RootChecks includes the claim-liveness root) and TestResourcePreflightFailsWhenClaimLivenessRootBelowHardInodeMinimum (proves Check() returns a ResourceExhaustedError when that root's free inodes are below the hard minimum, using the existing RootProbe override seam) in cli/internal/agent/execution_resources_test.go.
4. Run `cd cli && go test ./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/... -run "TestResourcePreflightIncludesClaimLivenessRoot|TestResourcePreflightFailsWhenClaimLivenessRootBelowHardInodeMinimum"`, then the full `./internal/bead/... ./internal/agent/...` suite, then `lefthook run pre-commit`, then commit.
</content>
